The command is executed directly with exec(), which can be dangerous if build() incorporates untrusted input without proper sanitization. There are no explicit hardcoded secrets, backdoors, or malicious network activity. The code relies on external build() logic, which is not shown, to produce the command string. If build() is secure, then this script is likely safe; otherwise, command injection risks are present. The code also uses a standard CLI pattern with no obfuscation or suspicious constructs.",
